title: Microbial cfDNA Library Preparation and Sequencing with Oxford Nanopore
author: Martin Schou Pedersen

steps_raw.instruction: Before starting, take out 96-well plate cold block, 2 mL tube cold block, and AmpureXP beads. Prepare all thermocycler programs beforehand.

In a PCR plate, prepare per sample: cfDNA (15–30 ng) 46 µL FFPE DNA Repair Buffer v2 7 µL NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Mix v2 2 µL. Mix 10× by pipetting and centrifuge plate briefly If normalizing between samples, add nuclease-free H₂O to bring all samples to 46 µL input.
Run thermocycler program cfDNA-repair: 37°C for 15 min (lid 50°C) → 4°C hold.
Centrifuge plate. Place on cold block. Add 2 µL NEBNext Proteinase K directly to each well. Mix 10× and centrifuge briefly.
Run thermocycler program cfDNA-proteinaseK: 37°C for 15 min (lid 75°C) → 65°C for 5 min → 4°C hold.
Centrifuge plate. Place on cold block. Add 3 µL NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix to each well. Mix 10× and centrifuge briefly.

Run thermocycler program cfDNA-end-prep: 20°C for 30 min (lid 75°C) → 65°C for 30 min → 4°C hold. NOTE: While incubating: label 2 mL LoBind tubes for next step, take out AmpureXP beads, and prepare 10 mL 80% ethanol (8 mL 100% EtOH + 2 mL water).
Remove end-prep plate from thermocycler and centrifuge briefly. Place on ice block until ready to cotinue.
Add 180 µL AmpureXP beads per sample into individual 2 mL LoBind tubes.
Add 60 µL of end-prepped DNA to the AmpureXP tube. Mix thoroughly by pipetting.
Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes, flicking/inverting approximately every minute.
Add 10 µL nuclease-free water. Gently resuspend with P10 or P50 pipette set to 7 µL.
Centrifuge briefly and place on magnet until liquid is clear.
Centrifuge briefly, return to magnet, and remove last traces of ethanol with a 10 µL tip.
Remove supernatant. Add 200 µL 80% ethanol. Remove supernatant. Repeat wash once more.
Transfer supernatant (containing DNA) to a new 2 mL LoBind tube. DNA can be stored at 4°C overnight before barcode ligation.
Centrifuge briefly and place on magnet for 1 minute.
Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes.
Thaw NEB Blunt/TA Ligase Master Mix, EDTA, and Native Barcodes. Keep on ice. Centrifuge Ligase Master Mix and barcodes briefly.
Mix Ligase Master Mix by pipetting. Add 10 µL to each 2 mL LoBind tube.
Centrifuge briefly. Place on magnet for at least 5 minutes until liquid is clear
Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes.

Pool all barcoded samples into one 2 mL LoBind tube. Add 3× AmpureXP volume per sample (e.g., 6 samples = 360 µL beads: (20 µL × 3) × 6). ONT's later protocols suggests only 1.2× AmpureXP. Microbial cfDNA is historically shorter than human, why we have chosen a higher AmpureXP ratio.
Add 7.5 µL eluted DNA to each tube. Add 2.5 µL of a unique barcode (NB01–NB96) to each tube. Gently mix reactions with P50/P100 pipette set to 12 µL. Record which barcode is used for each sample.
Incubate at room temperature for 20 minutes. While incubating, set Thermomixer to 37°C.
Add 4 µL EDTA (blue cap) to each reaction and mix.
Add 35 µL nuclease-free water. Gently resuspend with P50 pipette set to 25 µL.
Remove supernatant. Centrifuge briefly and return to magnet. Remove last traces of supernatan with a 10 µL tip.
Remove supernatant. Add 600 µL SFB. Wait 30 seconds.
Remove supernatant. Add 600 µL SFB. Wait 30 seconds.

Measure DNA concentration with Qubit (2 µL input). Expected yield: 15–40 ng total. For guidance please refer to: Josh Quick 2025. DNA Quantification using the Qubit Fluorometer. protocols.iohttps://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.261gekdeyg47/v1
Transfer supernatant to a new 2 mL LoBind tube.
Centrifuge briefly and place on magnet for 1 minute.
Incubate at 37°C on Thermomixer for 10 minutes, flicking tube gently for ~10 seconds every 2 minutes.
Thaw and place on ice block: NEBNext Quick Ligation Buffer, SFB, Quick T4 DNA Ligase, Native Adapter (NA), Elution Buffer (EB). Vortex Ligation Buffer for up to 39 seconds before use.
DNA may be stored at 4°C overnight before starting adaptor ligation if needed.
In a 2 mL LoBind tube, combine: 30 µL, Barcoded pool 5 µL, Native adapter (NA) 10 µL, NEBNext Quick Ligation Buffer 5 µL, Quick T4 DNA Ligase
Incubate at room temperature for 20 minutes.
Add 150 µL AmpureXP beads (3x). Mix by pipetting. Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes. Set Thermomixer to 37°C
Centrifuge briefly and place on magnet until liquid is clear.
Remove supernatant and take tube off the magnet. Add 125 µL SFB and resuspend. Centrifuge briefly and place on magnet until liquid is clear.
Repeat again: Remove supernatant and take tube off the magnet. Add 125 µL SFB and resuspend. Centrifuge briefly and place on magnet until liquid is clear.
Remove supernatant. Use a P10 pipette tip to remove the last traces.
Take the tube off the magnet. Add 34 µL EB (black cap) and resuspend.
Incubate tube at 37°C on the Thermomixer for 10 minutes, flicking gently every 2 minutes.
Centrifuge briefly and place on magnet for at least 1 minute.

Measure DNA with Qubit (2 µL input). Expected yield: ~10 ng total (0.4 ng/µL) For guidance please refer to: Josh Quick 2025. DNA Quantification using the Qubit Fluorometer. protocols.iohttps://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.261gekdeyg47/v1
Transfer supernatant (finished library) to a new 2 mL LoBind tube labeled 'cfDNA library' with today's date on the cap.
Store library at 4°C until loading.
Allow a PromethION flow cell to reach room temperature for at least 20 minutes. Thaw, mix, and keep on ice: Sequencing Buffer (SB), Library Beads (LIB), Flow Cell Tether (FCT), Flow Cell Flush (FCF).
Prepare priming mix: add 30 µL FCT to a new FCF tube (1170 µL) for each flow cell to be started. Keep on ice

Insert flow cell into PromethION 2 Solo. Start flow cell check in MinKNOW. Check flow cell for condensation and remove any liquid. Verify heat pad is visually intact. Flow cells must have >5000 pores or must be replaced by Oxford Nanopore. Report within 2 days if below threshold.
Wait at least 20 minutes before starting the sequencing
Load 200 µL loading mix with a P1000. Close port and attach light shield.
Load 500 µL priming mix to complete priming.
Prepare loading mix in a 2 mL LoBind tube: 100 µL, SB 68 µL, LIB 32 µL, Prepared library
Load 500 µL priming mix and wait 5 minutes.

Set a P1000 pipette to 200 µL. Remove air from port by inserting the tip and turning the pipet volumen to 220 µL gently. Visual flow cell loading guides can be found at the Oxford Nanopore community. https://www.welcome.nanoporetech.com/
Store library at 4°C until loading.Open the priming port (clockwise).

The MinKNOW interface has changed multiple times so giving a detailed description of the setup is difficult but start the sequencing run with the following settings added: Super-accurate Basecalling (SUP) Do not remove short reads with a filter PHRED score > 10 Run duration: 96 hours
